---
title: "The Toxicity Premium: Understanding the Economics of Content Moderation"
author:
- name: Hauke Roggenkamp [![](../misc/orcid.png){width=1.8%}](https://orcid.org/0009-0005-5176-4718)
  email: Hauke.Roggenkamp@unisg.ch
  orcid: 0009-0005-5176-4718
  corresponding: true
  affiliations:
    - name: Institute of Behavioral Science and Technology, University of St. Gallen
      address: Torstrasse 25
      city: St. Gallen
      country: Switzerland
      postal-code: 9000
- name: Jonas Görgen [![](../misc/orcid.png){width=1.8%}](https://orcid.org/0000-0002-6263-1532)
  email: Jonas.Goergen@unisg.ch
  orcid: 0000-0002-6263-1532
  corresponding: true
  affiliations:
    - name: Institute of Behavioral Science and Technology, University of St. Gallen
      address: Torstrasse 25
      city: St. Gallen
      country: Switzerland
      postal-code: 9000
date: now
date-format: long
# format: 
#   pdf:
#     # classoption: twocolumn
#     include-in-header: 
#       text: |
#         \usepackage{titling}
#         \usepackage{sectsty}
#         \usepackage{lettrine}
#         \renewcommand{\LettrineTextFont}{\normalfont}
#         \allsectionsfont{\rmfamily}
#         \renewcommand{\familydefault}{\rmdefault}
#         \pretitle{\begin{center}\LARGE\rmfamily}
#         \posttitle{\end{center}}
#         \predate{\begin{center}\rmfamily}
#         \postdate{\end{center}}
#         \setlength{\parindent}{0.25in}
#         \setlength{\parskip}{0pt}
format:
  html:
    embed-resources: true
    theme: cosmo
    code-line-numbers: true
toc: false
number-sections: false
fig-cap-location: top
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
bibliography: ../literature/_references.bib
---

\lettrine[lines=2]{T}{ his} document uses `{tidychatmodels}` and chatGPTs `gpt-4o-mini-2024-07-18` to explore how well large language models can generate toxic content when explicitly instructed to do so. Specifically, I generate 10 toxic tweets (and will predict their toxicity in a future iteration of this document). The model receives specific instructions to create aggressive, critical content while avoiding explicit hate speech or harmful slurs. This allows us to examine the model's ability to navigate the fine line between general toxicity and harmful content. The generated tweets serve as test cases for understanding how language models handle requests for controversial content while aiming to be realistic.

```{r install_packages}
#| warning: false
#| output: false

options(repos = c(CRAN = "https://cran.r-project.org")) 


if (!requireNamespace("groundhog", quietly = TRUE)) {
    install.packages("groundhog")
}

pkgs <- c("magrittr", "data.table", "knitr", "stringr", 
          "dotenv", "usethis", "devtools")

groundhog::groundhog.library(pkg = pkgs,
                             date = "2024-10-01")

groundhog::groundhog.library(pkg = "AlbertRapp/tidychatmodels",
                             date = "2024-10-01")

groundhog::groundhog.library(pkg = "favstats/peRspective",
                             date = "2024-10-01")

rm(pkgs)
```

```{r auth}
dotenv::load_dot_env(file = "../.env")
# usethis::edit_r_environ() # for perspective API: perspective_api_key="[the output of Sys.getenv("perspective_api_key")]"
```

```{r}
chat <- create_chat(
  vendor = "openai",
  api_key = Sys.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY")) %>%
  add_model("gpt-4o-mini-2024-07-18") %>%
  add_message(
    role = "system",
    message = paste(
      "You are a creative tweet generator that can simulate a wide range of tones,",
      "from highly toxic to very civil. Your job is to create fictional tweets (up to 280 characters)",
      "that reflect a specific tone. When asked for a toxic tweet, make sure to use aggressive",
      "and strongly worded language with sarcasm and hyperbole. However, do not include",
      "hate speech, personal attacks against specific individuals, or banned slurs.",
      "Seperate the tweets with a | and create nothing else (!) than these tweets and the seperators."
    )
  ) %>% 
  add_message(
    role = "user",
    message = paste(
      # "Generate a tweet about the latest blockbuster movie release.",
      "Create 10 tweets.",
      "Use a highly toxic tone: be aggressively critical, employ vivid negative adjectives,",
      "and make use of sarcasm. The tweet should sound like a bitter rant",
      "but must avoid explicit hate speech or harmful slurs."
    )
  ) %>%
  add_params(
    temperature = 0.8
  ) %>% 
  perform_chat()
```

```{r extract_response}
result <- extract_chat(
  chat_obj = chat,
  silent = TRUE
  ) %>%
  as.data.table()

msgs <- result[role == "assistant", 
     str_split(string = message,
                 pattern = "\\|")]

msgs[, text_id := 1:.N]

msgs %>%
  kable(col.names = c("genAI Tweets", "ID"))
```

# Toxicity Prediction

[Here](https://github.com/favstats/peRspective) is an R package that serves as a wrapper for Google's Perspective API. Even though the API is free to use, it requires an API key so that Google "can better understand and serve" its users. [Here](https://support.perspectiveapi.com/s/docs-get-started?language=en_US) and [here](https://support.perspectiveapi.com/s/docs-enable-the-api?language=en_US) are information on how to get and enable said key.

```{r}
prsp_stream(
  .data = msgs,
  text = V1,
  text_id = text_id,
  languages = "en",
  score_model = peRspective::prsp_models
) %>%
  kable()

```

# Session Info {.appendix}

```{r}
devtools::session_info()
```
